task: Write a Python function collapse_delims(s) that splits the string on commas, treats any run of consecutive commas as one split, drops empty pieces at the start and end, and returns the pieces rejoined with a single comma between each. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function passes all criteria with only minor room for improvement in readability.
trace: I approached this task by first replacing any double commas with a single comma to simplify the splitting process. Then, I used list comprehension to select only the non-empty pieces from the split string and finally re-joined them using a single comma between each. To handle an edge case where there might be extra leading or trailing empty strings due to consecutive commas, I made sure to exclude those while joining the final result.
